instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/investments/account-aggregators-vs-manual-uploads-life-insurance.html
Account Aggregators vs Manual Uploads: What’s Better for Life Insurance Customers?
Applying for life insurance requires sharing key financial and personal documents, such as income proof, identity details, and bank statements. One way to do so is through manual uploads—scanned copies or physical submissions of documents. But with the introduction of the Account Aggregator (AA) framework, customers also have a digital, secure, and seamless alternative to share data directly from financial institutions to insurers. Read More
Let’s understand both options across key aspects like speed, security and convenience. This will help you understand which approach aligns best with your needs. Read Less
Ease of Document SubmissionManual Uploads:Customers are required to scan and upload documents like payslips, and bank statements.
Account Aggregators:You can share verified data directly from your bank or financial institution in just a few clicks. The process is paperless and avoids the need to track, scan, or convert files.
Data Accuracy and VerificationManual Uploads:Insurers review submitted documents manually.
Account Aggregators:Data is fetched directly from regulated financial sources..
Security and PrivacyManual Uploads:Uploaded files are stored on multiple systems or email servers.
Account Aggregators:The AA framework is governed by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). Data is end-to-end encrypted and never stored by the aggregator.
Eligibility Across Customer ProfilesManual Uploads:This method works universally, even for those who don’t have digital access or are uncomfortable with online platforms.
Account Aggregators:While growing in adoption, it may still require basic digital literacy and access to internet-enabled devices.
Operational MethodManual Uploads:Customers are required to complete tasks such as visiting branches, scanning documents, or obtaining notary attestations in certain cases.
Account Aggregators:This approach streamlines the process by eliminating physical document handling, enabling digital verification and reducing manual intervention.
Final ThoughtsData sharing in financial services can be completed through both manual and digital processes, each involving specific steps and requirements. With the growing adoption of digital frameworks, solutions like Account Aggregators are being introduced to enable secure and structured data sharing. Customers can check with their service providers to understand the available options and choose what works best for them.
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited). assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.
